description: Hardened Recall@k dataset for the SS6 RAG retriever over the eleven-7 codebase. 'queries' are positives (each lists the file(s) that genuinely answer it); many are paraphrased to share little/no vocabulary with the target, and the repo contains look-alike files (snsClient vs smsClient, orderRepository vs productRepository, money.ts vs pricing.ts, Card vs MetricTile vs Badge) that the right answer must beat. 'negatives' are hard negatives whose answer is ABSENT from the repo — a confident hit is a false positive. Paths are relative to project root.

queries.query: how is a delivery fee worked out from the cart total and pickup vs delivery?
turning an integer satang amount into a readable baht string
writing an order and its line items together so they can't half-save
queries.note: paraphrase of transactional insert; must beat productRepository

queries.query: the one wrapper every Amazon text-message to a phone number goes through
pushing a job onto the queue and polling messages back off it
single source of palette, spacing and font-size constants for the UI
every backend call to the database is funneled through one connection pool
which UI building blocks must I reuse before making new ones?
picking a free rider in the right zone and guessing how many minutes the trip takes
the only place the Angular app is allowed to make HTTP calls
round badge that shows a person's initials
the small coloured pill that flags an order or courier status
queries.note: must beat the MetricTile and Card primitives

queries.query: the background consumer that assigns a courier and texts the customer it's on the way
where the tables, money columns and foreign keys for orders are defined
applying a coupon code and capping the percentage discount
issuing a partial refund and recording the payment lifecycle event
moving stock from one branch to another and alerting when one runs low
the product codenames mapped to each internal microservice

negatives.query: how does the GraphQL subscription for live order updates get set up?
the biometric / fingerprint login screen for the courier app
Kubernetes horizontal pod autoscaler configuration for the API
the Redis write-through cache layer in front of MySQL
